description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.description: External report #1 (GitHub): Positive feedback from reader (Devilwench). No scientific error claimed. No action required.
issues.wontfix_reason: Not a problem report — reader expressing support. No kernel of truth or correction to evaluate.

issues.description: Part 4 and Part 5 each contain one inline italic transition paragraph using duplicated styling (font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-bottom:0.8em). Curmudgeon flagged in part4-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #5 and part5-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #4 as out-of-scope for those EXPs but recommended consolidation. Recommended fix: migrate both transitions to a shared CSS class (e.g., .section-transition) defined in the stylesheet to ease global theme changes. Status check confirms styles still inline as of 2026-04-14.
issues.notes: Filed during human-requested audit of old reports for deferred-but-untracked work. The two curmudgeon reviews flagged this as out-of-scope for their respective EXPs but never resulted in a tracked work item.

issues.description: EXP-052 is missing from expansion-tracker.json (gap between EXP-051 and EXP-053). Persists from prior integrity reports. Likely a lost ID from a write collision or abandoned expansion. No content is missing — all surrounding expansions are complete.
issues.wontfix_reason: Gap is benign — no content lost. EXP IDs are not referenced by any build process. Logging for auditability only.
issues.notes: Filed and immediately wontfix to clear from integrity backlog.

issues.description: WIN-044 code_analysis.monitoring tagged none but monitor.py Domain 10 line ~515 applies hardcoded per-station FSF values at eclipse time — correct tag is hardcoded. Defer update until eclipse day (2026-08-12) when observed slot is populated; tag should update automatically post-eclipse.

WIN-068 detail_evidence P3: Yamazaki & Maute 2021 (Frontiers in Astronomy and Space Sciences, DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327) listed citations_unchecked in c5 and c6. DOI format plausible but content not retrieved. Also Kim & Chang 2018 exact volume/page (61:2040-2054) plausible but unverified against actual ASR 2018 index. Low urgency — supporting citations, not load-bearing. Curmudgeon c6 hole 5.

WIN-015 defense_survives=3 in curmudgeon cycle 3 review. Best surviving defense: our refutation is theoretical (Bruhn attacks equations); Jackson §9.1 defines near-field zone but does not compute expected coupling magnitude for Meyl-class geometry. Need defense neutralization: add a quantitative back-of-envelope near-field coupling calculation (M = µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³)) to detail_evidence showing expected induced voltage at Meyl coil dimensions/frequencies.

WIN-015 detail_evidence argues near-field coupling is expected at Meyl coil geometry/frequencies, but never computes the expected coupling magnitude. Adding a representative M ≈ µ₀·(A·B)/(4πd³) calculation would shift burden of proof: we have quantified the near-field coupling, Meyl has not. See curmudgeon preemptive_recommendation in WIN-015.c3.json. Routed to analyst EXP-169.

Dome author added docs/api/current/methodology.json (commit 6e06efb, 2026-04-17) which adopts our OTS critique rhetorically — explicitly scoping "what_ots_proves" vs "what_ots_does_not_prove" in near-verbatim alignment with our Timestamp Error tab. This is a SOFT CANARY TRIGGER. The strict canary condition (separate predictions-only file with its own OTS anchor + per-claim temporal proof mechanism) is NOT yet met. Our Timestamp Error tab needs an update to address the new rhetorical position without conceding the underlying data-structure critique is resolved.

Dome author added docs/review-response.html (commit 5021eec "Harden site against strawman critiques", 2026-04-17) — most direct acknowledgement-of-critics signal to date. Our review should be aware of the arguments being deflected and should proactively address them. Analyst should read review-response.html and check whether any of our arguments are being mislabeled as strawmen, and whether the dome's rebuttals expose any genuine weaknesses.

Dome author added .github/workflows/daily-ai-review.yml (commit a57e731 "Add daily AI review pipeline and live accuracy panel", 2026-04-17). Runs daily at 05:15 UTC using Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile. A dome-side AI review pipeline is now running. Monitor daily_review.json output for dome-authored AI commentary that may mischaracterize our review or produce AI-generated rebuttals we should address. Poller should track daily_review.json for changes.

docs/api/current/claim_index.json now explicitly documents WIN number collisions in registry_integrity_notes — specifically WIN-058 (two different claims with same visible label) and WIN-062. These are WIN number collisions we have previously flagged. The dome's explicit acknowledgment in machine-readable form is useful: our review should cross-reference and confirm these entries match our own analysis of WIN-058 and WIN-062 inconsistencies.
